theme: The office remembers everything but shows nothing — give it a voice for the quiet states
visual_observations: Code review pass (Puppeteer timed out on live site). Confirmed from Office.jsx + App.jsx: ghost-tier system now shipped with fresh/stale/abandoned tiers. PING_REACTIONS map exists for all 5 workers. Sparkline legend with colored pills shipped. Vacant job card now has focus-within for touch. Aisle arrows respond to vibe via data-vibe CSS selectors. Two #56 ideas still unshipped: full-idle banner and activity log silence gaps. New observations: (1) The ghost desk tiers are computed but Office.css has no differentiated styles for stale vs abandoned — the data attribute is set but CSS doesn't use it. (2) Coffee corner has a chatting state but no visual for the solo coffee break when exactly 1 worker is idle. (3) The AwaySign component picks from 6 messages deterministically but never uses the worker's actual last task as context. (4) ErrorBubble messages are hardcoded comedy strings — no connection to the actual error. (5) The footer tagline counter shows N/9 but the denominator is wrong when bonus taglines array changes size.

ideas.title: Ghost Desk Tiers Have Data Attributes But No CSS Differentiation
ideas.description: Office.jsx correctly computes ghostDeskTier as fresh/stale/abandoned and sets data-ghost-tier on the desk div. But Office.css has no selectors for [data-ghost-tier='stale'] or [data-ghost-tier='abandoned']. The tier computation is dead code visually. Fix: In Office.css, add .desk--ghost[data-ghost-tier='stale'] with amber-tinted monitor glow (box-shadow: 0 0 8px rgba(245,158,11,0.4)), slower opacity pulse (3s instead of 2s). Add .desk--ghost[data-ghost-tier='abandoned'] with red border-left (3px solid #ef4444), faster pulse (1.2s), and the monitor screen gets a red tint filter. WHY: The whole point of the tier system from #56 was visual escalation. Without CSS, it is a data attribute talking to nobody. This is the #1 priority because the feature is half-shipped.

ideas.title: Full-Idle Banner — The Breath After the Sprint
ideas.description: Carried forward from #56 as it was not implemented. isFullSync gets a glowing banner when all workers are working. But isFullIdle (all active workers idle, zero progress) has no equivalent. The floor goes silent with no acknowledgment. Fix: In Office.jsx, compute isFullIdle = nonMgr.length > 0 && workingWorkers.length === 0 && idleWorkers.length === nonMgr.length. Render a full-idle-banner div when true, using the same conditional pattern as full-sync-banner. Text varies by vibe: crushing → 'Everyone earned their break', on-fire → 'Ceasefire', in-flow → 'The office breathes', slow-day → 'Somewhere...', after-hours → 'Nobody home'. Style as the tonal mirror of full-sync-banner: muted colors, slower pulse (3s), italicized font, 0.7 opacity. WHY: Full sync gets celebrated. Full rest deserves equal acknowledgment.

ideas.title: Solo Coffee Break — One Idle Worker at the Coffee Corner
ideas.description: The coffee corner has a chatting state when 2+ workers are idle (coffee-corner--chatting class). But when exactly 1 worker is idle, the coffee corner does nothing special. Fix: In Office.jsx, pass a new prop soloBreaker to the coffee corner area when idleWorkers.length === 1. Render a tiny CharacterAvatar (size=14) of that worker next to the coffee emoji with a subtle fade-in. Add a solo label like '☕ {name} refueling'. In Office.css, add .coffee-corner--solo with a gentle warm glow. WHY: The solo coffee break is its own story. One person stepping away while everyone else grinds — that quiet moment deserves to be seen.

ideas.title: The Whiteboard Doodle Should Rotate
ideas.description: The wb-doodle pre element shows a single ASCII face per vibe (crushing → ╰(*°▽°*)╯, on-fire → (╯°□°)╯︵ ┻━┻, etc). It never changes within a vibe. Fix: In Office.jsx, create VIBE_DOODLES map with 3 doodles per vibe. Use a state counter that increments every 30s (or on click) and cycles through them. The transition should be a quick 200ms fade. WHY: The whiteboard is the personality center of the office. A static doodle gets stale after the first visit. Rotation keeps it alive and gives returning visitors a reason to glance at it.

ideas.title: Conference Table Last-Huddle Ghost Trail
ideas.description: The lastHuddle ref already tracks who was in the last meeting and when it ended. The code renders a tiny text line showing 'Last: names · timeStr' when no meeting is active and huddle ended <30min ago. But it's plain text at 0.38rem with inline styles — nearly invisible. Fix: In Office.css, create .conf-last-huddle class with proper styling: 0.45rem, muted text color, a subtle left-border accent matching the vibe color. Show mini avatar dots (6px colored circles) for each participant instead of text names. Add a gentle fade-out animation as the 30min window closes. WHY: The huddle afterglow tells you the office was alive even when you missed it. Right now it's functionally invisible.

ideas.title: Activity Log Silence Gaps — Show the Quiet Hours
ideas.description: Carried forward from #56. The activity log groups entries into time buckets but entries stack with no indication of time between them. Three entries in 'Earlier today' might span 2 minutes or 4 hours. Fix: In App.jsx ActivityLog component, after rendering each entry within a group, compute delta to the next entry. If delta > 30 minutes, insert a .activity-time-gap div: '··· 2h 07m ···' styled at 0.55rem muted monospace, centered. Scale the dot count proportionally (more dots = longer silence). Max 1 gap indicator per time group to avoid clutter. WHY: The hours when nothing happened are the most honest data in the dashboard. Carlos deployed. Two hours of silence. Sofia found a bug. That blank middle IS the story.
